rationale: Both papers centrally examine how word-of-mouth/review language shapes persuasion outcomes: the citing paper studies positive emotional language effects on helpfulness/favorability, while the cited paper shows endorsement wording (explicit vs. implicit) changes persuasion via inferred expertise/liking. Given this close conceptual overlap and the citing paper’s stated extension of prior WOM language research, this citation is plausibly foundational/comparative rather than an obvious incidental mention.
